title: Use local contest ranking and AUC-safe blending instead of generic calibration
summary: Pit decisions are often relative contests against nearby cars; F1 strategy notes undercut/overcut usually matters only when rivals are within striking range.
rationale: Pit decisions are often relative contests against nearby cars; F1 strategy notes undercut/overcut usually matters only when rivals are within striking range. For ROC AUC, monotone post-calibration adds little, and scikit-learn notes isotonic can even change AUC by introducing ties.
implementation_hint: Create race-lap groups and train a CatBoost pairwise/query model only on plausible contest pairs, e.g. nearby position or small lap-time-gap proxies, then blend its OOF percentile ranks with the main classifier and survival model. If post-processing is used, prefer sigmoid or pure rank averaging over isotonic.
expected_effect: Should improve ordering among ambiguous same-lap candidates while keeping ensemble gains AUC-focused rather than probability-focused.
risk: Pair construction may be noisy without exact gap features, and an over-aggressive query model can hurt isolated obvious cases.
